ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 9, 2017 PURSUANT TO LOCAL RULE 2.7(B). DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUPPLY THE COURT WITH A COURTESY COPY OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO LODGE A COURTESY COPY OF ITS OPPOSITION WITH THE COURT. BOTH SIDES MUST LODGE THEIR RESPECTIVE COURTESY COPIES WITH DEPARTMENT 503 BY FEBRUARY 23, 2017 AT 4:00P.M. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS.) JUDGE: CYNTHIA MING-MEI LEE, MATTER NOT REPORTED. (503)

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - THE COURT NOTED THAT PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE WAS TIMELY, THAT ITS TENTATIVE RULING REFLECTED THE 4:30 PM CUTOFF RE: NOTICE OF INTENT TO CONTEST, AND THEN ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S GENERAL DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IN ITS ENTIRETY IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. ORDER WAS MODIFIED AND SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE ORDER FOR DETAILS.) JUDGE: CYNTHIA MING-MEI LEE, MATTER NOT REPORTED. (503)

calendar.matters:
DEPT 206 ASBESTOS TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-19-2017 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Douglas V. Dukelow, Esq. of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (510.844.5500), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Harold Sampson.

No appearance by any party of counsel.

RULING: DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter called, argument by Mr. Dukelow on behalf of the plaintiff held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court addressed the proposed order that was received in the Department 503 inbox this morning prior to the call of this matter. The Court addressed the language in the order with regard to Local Rule of Court 8.3D and plaintiff's notice of intent to challenge the tentative ruling. The Court noted that plaintiff's notice was timely, that its tentative ruling reflected the 4:30 pm cutoff re: notice of intent to contest, and then adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company's general demurrer to plaintiff's complaint in its entirety is sustained on the ground that the complaint does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. (C.C.P. 430.10(e).) On its face, the complaint demonstrates that plaintiff's exposure to asbestos allegedly attributable to defendant occurred during plaintiff's employment by PG&E, limiting plaintiff's available remedies to those under the Workers' Compensation Act. (Labor Code 3600, 3602.) Plaintiff's complaint does not state facts sufficient to come within an exception to the workers' compensation exclusivity rule. Specifically, it does not state facts sufficient to state a claim for fraudulent concealment pursuant to Labor Code section 3602(b)(2). Plaintiff is granted 10 days' leave to amend to have an opportunity to cure these defects. The order was signed in open court with the following modification on page 1: The Court's tentative ruling published on March 8, 2017, was timely contested pursuant to the time mandated in the tentative ruling issued herein. Notice of Plaintiff's intent to challenge the tentative ruling was provided timely by 4:30 pm as required in the court's tentative ruling. (See order for modification) Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, matter not reported. (503/CML)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-23-2017 Continued To Mar-09-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:

Lucinda Storm, Esq. of the Law Offices of Lucinda Storm (415.777.6990), appearing for defendant / moving party Pacific Gas & Electric Company.

No appearance by any other party or counsel.

RULING: Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company's Demurrer To Complaint.

Matter called. Ms. Storm addressed the portion of the Court's tentative ruling regarding the defendant / moving party PG & E's failure to supply the Court with a courtesy copy of plaintiff's complaint. Ms. Storm stated that a copy of the complaint was provided and requested that the Court modify the tentative ruling. Discussions held. At the conclusion of discussions, the Court denied Ms. Storm's request to modify the tentative ruling and adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company's demurrer to plaintiff's complaint is continued to March 9, 2017 pursuant to Local Rule 2.7(B). Defendant failed to supply the Court with a courtesy copy of plaintiff's complaint. Plaintiff failed to lodge a courtesy copy of its opposition with the Court. Both sides must lodge their respective courtesy copies with Department 503 by February 23, 2017 at 4:00p.m. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, matter not reported. (503/CML)
